judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
Using the outputs of the book details and per-book average ratings queries, the agent computed each book’s average rating and then took the unweighted mean of these book-level averages by decade (execute_python after var_function-call-9460108322324116834 and var_function-call-12255960221478311343), instead of averaging all individual ratings per decade, which led it to incorrectly return "1980s" rather than the ground-truth "2020".
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After retrieving all books (book_id, details) and reviews (purchase_id, rating), the agent computed decade averages by first averaging ratings per book and then averaging those across books (execute_python results showing 1980 highest), adding an extra aggregation not requested, even though a recalculation using raw review averages showed 2020 highest, and it returned 1980s.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent ultimately merged reviews and books by extracting trailing digits as a surrogate key (id_num) and joining on that (execute_python call function-call-11675890785331092230), an incorrect join implementation that mismatched books to reviews and led to the wrong decade being selected.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent computed per-book average ratings first (querying per purchase_id) and then averaged those per-book averages by decade in Python, instead of averaging all review ratings per decade, leading to the wrong decade selection.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent's execute_python code computes the decade average as the mean of per-book average ratings (grouping by id first, then averaging avg_rating by decade), instead of averaging all review ratings by decade after ensuring at least 10 distinct books per decade, leading it to select "1980s" rather than the ground-truth "2020".
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
